qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Stored XSS via unsanitized innerHTML in HTML string conversion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same library (html2pdf.js), same root cause (unsanitized text source assigned to innerHTML without proper sanitization in worker.js:71 and utils.js:18-24), same vulnerability class (XSS/CWE-79), and same attack vector (HTML string with event handler passed to html2pdf.from() or html2pdf()). Aligns with advisory issue #865, fix PR #877, and commit 988826e.

selected_finding.title: Stored XSS via unsanitized innerHTML in HTML string conversion

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The finding correctly identifies the advisory-described vulnerability: html2pdf.js accepts HTML strings via from() or direct invocation and assigns them to innerHTML without sanitization (only removing <script> tags), allowing XSS through event handlers. The code locations (worker.js:71, utils.js:18-24), attack vector, and root cause align with the advisory (GHSA-w8x4-x68c-m6fc, CVE-2026-22787, fix in v0.14.0 via DOMPurify sanitization). However, the PoC could not be executed because the environment lacks network connectivity to clone the target repository from GitHub (exit code 128) and Docker Hub is also unreachable, preventing both native and containerized reproduction. The finding is supported by strong static analysis evidence from the source code references.
evidence: vulnerability_report.json identifies worker.js:71 and utils.js:18-24 as the vulnerable code locations, matching the advisory's described unsanitized innerHTML assignment path
The exploit path matches the advisory example: attacker-controlled HTML string -> html2pdf.from(src, 'string') -> createElement('div', {innerHTML: src}) -> event handler execution
The finding's attack vector (img onerror bypassing script-tag-only removal) is consistent with the advisory's described vulnerability class
poc_report.json claims successful prior reproduction with Docker, but the judge execution failed due to network connectivity (exit code 128, git clone failure)
The advisory fix (v0.14.0 adding DOMPurify sanitization) directly addresses the root cause identified in the finding
blockers_to_higher_tier: Network connectivity to github.com and Docker Hub is unavailable, preventing the PoC from cloning the target repository and building the reproduction environment
No runtime artifacts were produced that satisfy the exploit success or bug activation predicates
Docker build fails due to inability to pull ubuntu:22.04 base image, npm install fails due to cache corruption and network issues

poc_executions.observed_effect: PoC execution failed at the git clone step (exit code 128) because the execution environment cannot reach github.com. The run.sh script attempts to clone https://github.com/eKoopmans/html2pdf.js.git which times out after 55 seconds. Docker Hub and npm registry are also unreachable in this environment. No vulnerable code was activated.
